prerequisites: A running MySQL server reachable from the connector, with its host and port known (default MySQL port is 3306).
A target database (schema) that already exists on the server, supplied in the `database` field.
A MySQL user account (`username` plus `password`) created with CREATE USER and granted the privileges needed to write to the target database via GRANT.
Network access from the connector to the MySQL host; if the server is not directly reachable, an SSH bastion host for the optional `tunnel_method` SSH tunnel.
If you enable `ssl`, a MySQL server configured for encrypted (TLS) connections.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to MySQL with a database account. Set `host`, `port`, `database`, and `username`, and provide the account `password` as the secret. The account must hold privileges (granted via GRANT) sufficient to create and write to tables in the target database.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (optional)
authMethods.summary: Reach a MySQL server that is not directly accessible by routing the connection through an SSH bastion using `tunnel_method`. The tunnel authenticates either with an SSH private key (`tunnel_method.ssh_key`) or with the SSH user's password (`tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`). This authenticates the SSH hop only; the MySQL username/password is still required for the database itself.

setupSteps.title: Create a MySQL user account
setupSteps.body: On the MySQL server, create the account the connector will use, for example `CREATE USER 'username'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'password';`. Use this value for the `username` field and the `password` secret. See the MySQL manual on adding accounts.

setupSteps.title: Grant write privileges on the target database
setupSteps.body: Grant the account the privileges it needs on the database named in the `database` field using the GRANT statement, then run FLUSH PRIVILEGES. As a destination the account must be able to create and modify tables and write rows in that database.

setupSteps.title: Enter connection details
setupSteps.body: Provide the required `host`, `port`, `database`, and `username` fields, plus the `password` secret. Optionally set `jdbc_url_params` to append extra JDBC connection parameters and `raw_data_schema` / `disable_type_dedupe` to control how loaded data is staged and deduplicated.

setupSteps.title: Configure SSL (optional)
setupSteps.body: Enable the `ssl` option to connect over an encrypted (TLS) connection. The MySQL server must be configured to support encrypted connections; see the MySQL manual on configuring encrypted connections.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the MySQL server is not directly reachable, set `tunnel_method` to connect through an SSH bastion. Authenticate the tunnel with either an SSH private key (`tunnel_method.ssh_key`) or the SSH user's password (`tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`).

sources.title: MySQL 8.0 Reference Manual: Adding Accounts, Assigning Privileges, and Dropping Accounts
MySQL 8.0 Reference Manual: Access Control and Account Management
MySQL Reference Manual: Configuring MySQL to Use Encrypted Connections
